name: BlueLinx Holdings Inc.
business: BlueLinx is a leading wholesale distributor of residential and commercial building products in the United States. It distributes specialty products (engineered wood, siding, millwork, outdoor living products, specialty lumber and panels, industrial products) and structural products (lumber, plywood, oriented strand board, rebar, remesh) to dealers, national home centers, pro dealers, cooperatives, specialty distributors, regional and local dealers, and industrial manufacturers.

exposures.sentence: We estimate that demand from the residential repair and remodel market ( R&R ) accounts for approximately 45 percent of our annual sales.
We supply products to a broad base of customers including national home centers, pro dealers, cooperatives, specialty distributors, regional and local dealers and industrial manufacturers.

We also provide a wide range of value-added services and solutions aimed at relieving distribution and logistics challenges for our customers and suppliers, while enhancing their marketing and inventory management capabilities.
we are in the early stages of integrating artificial intelligence ( AI ) into our business to support our business operations.

continuing technology initiatives associated with our digital transformation, a multi-year initiative aimed at modernizing and integrating our core technologies by improving data quality, strengthening transportation management and operational systems, and digitizing key processes.

catalysts.event: Renegotiation of five collective bargaining agreements covering approximately four percent of associates
catalysts.sentence: Five CBAs covering approximately four percent of our associates are up for renewal in fiscal year 2026, of which one is currently in the renegotiation process.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. homeowner improvements and repairs spending expected to be approximately $517 billion in 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: total U.S. homeowner improvements and repairs spending expected to be approximately $517 billion in 2026, compared to $511 billion, $501 billion, $510 billion, and $515 billion in 2025, 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively

tamClaims.claim: Housing shortage estimates range between 3.8 million and 4.7 million homes.
tamClaims.sentence: shortfall estimates generally range between 3.8 million and 4.7 million homes.
